other_observers: 5/18/06: also observed by Diane J. Brown. 5/20/06: also heard by Bill Keampfer.; I am the original observer.
specific_location: Arapaho Ranch, along Middle Boulder Creek
description: Bird was yellow underneath and green-yellow on upperparts. Black streaks on sides of body. Black lines on sides of neck. Black lines on head; one through the eye and one below the eye, that merged.Behaviors: Singing, perched near top of birch, alders, and willows. Moved from shrub to shrub, generally staying high and singing.Call: It was singing all 3 times encountered. The song first lead me to believe that it was a different warbler. I was not familier with the song. After 1st time heard, went home and listened to tapes and my memory matched the song of the prairie warbler. This was reconfirmed during the second and thrid trip.Plumage: adult
